Section.Text: The amount of the base unadjusted pensionable earnings of a contributor for a year determined under section 53 is to be adjusted for each year in which there is a division of unadjusted pensionable earnings under section 55 or 55.1 and under a provincial pension plan.

Section.MarginalNote: First additional unadjusted pensionable earnings for years of division
Section.Text: The amount of the first additional unadjusted pensionable earnings of a contributor for a year determined under section 53.1 is to be adjusted for each year in which there is a division of those earnings under section 55.1.

Section.MarginalNote: Second additional unadjusted pensionable earnings for years of division
Section.Text: The amount of the second additional unadjusted pensionable earnings of a contributor for a year determined under section 53.2 is to be adjusted for each year in which there is a division of those earnings under section 55.1.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 55.2(5) to (7) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Division of base unadjusted pensionable earnings
Subsection.Text: If there is a division under section 55.1, the base unadjusted pensionable earnings for each person subject to the division for the period of cohabitation attributable to base contributions made under this Act, determined in the same manner as the total pensionable earnings of a contributor attributable to base contributions made under this Act are determined in section 78, are to be added and then divided equally, and the base unadjusted pensionable earnings so divided are to be attributed to each person.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Division of first additional unadjusted pensionable earnings
Subsection.Text: If there is a division of first additional unadjusted pensionable earnings under section 55.1, those earnings for each person subject to the division for the period of cohabitation are to be added and then divided equally, and the first additional unadjusted pensionable earnings so divided are to be attributed to each person.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Division of second additional unadjusted pensionable earnings
Subsection.Text: If there is a division of second additional unadjusted pensionable earnings under section 55.1, those earnings for each person subject to the division for the period of cohabitation are to be added and then divided equally, and the second additional unadjusted pensionable earnings so divided are to be attributed to each person.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Effect of division of base unadjusted pensionable earnings
Subsection.Text: If there is a division of base unadjusted pensionable earnings under section 55.1 and under a provincial pension plan, for the purposes of benefit calculation and payment under this Act, the total unadjusted pensionable earnings of a contributor for a year of division are the aggregate of their base unadjusted pensionable earnings attributed under subsection (5) and their unadjusted pensionable earnings attributed under a provincial pension plan.

No division of base unadjusted pensionable earnings under section 55.1 is to be made for any month during which the persons subject to the division cohabited and for which either of them contributed to a provincial pension plan (and, for the purposes of this subsection, months during which the persons cohabited are to be determined in the prescribed manner), unless the unadjusted pensionable earnings attributed to the persons under the provincial pension plan are divided for that month in a manner substantially similar to that described in this section and section 55.1.
The portion of subsection 55.2(8) of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:
No division of base unadjusted pensionable earnings under section 55.1 for a period of cohabitation of the persons subject to the division is to be made

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for a year in which the total base unadjusted pensionable earnings of the persons do not exceed twice the Year’s Basic Exemption;

Subsection.Text: Section 55.2 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (8):

Subsection.MarginalNote: No division of first additional unadjusted pensionable earnings
Subsection.Text: No division of first additional unadjusted pensionable earnings under section 55.1 for a period of cohabitation of the persons subject to the division is to be made

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for a year in which the total first additional unadjusted pensionable earnings of the persons do not exceed twice the Year’s Basic Exemption;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the period before which one of the persons reached 18 years of age or after which one of the persons reached 70 years of age; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: for the period in which one of the persons was a beneficiary of a retirement pension under this Act.

Subsection.MarginalNote: No division of second additional unadjusted pensionable earnings
Subsection.Text: No division of second additional unadjusted pensionable earnings under section 55.1 for a period of cohabitation of the persons subject to the division is to be made

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for the period before which one of the persons reached 18 years of age or after which one of the persons reached 70 years of age; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the period in which one of the persons was a beneficiary of a retirement pension under this Act.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 56(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: 75% of the amount of the contributor’s retirement pension, which amount is calculated as the aggregate of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount calculated as provided in subsection (3),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount calculated as provided in subsection (3.1), and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount calculated as provided in subsection (3.2).

Subsection.Text: Subsection 56(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The amount of the portion of the contributor’s retirement pension that is to be used for the purpose of subparagraph (1)(b)(i) is equal to 25% of their average monthly pensionable earnings calculated as provided in subsections (4) and (5).

The amount of the portion of the contributor’s retirement pension that is to be used for the purpose of subparagraph (1)(b)(ii) is equal to 8.33% of their first additional monthly pensionable earnings calculated as provided in subsection (4.01).

The amount of the portion of the contributor’s retirement pension that is to be used for the purpose of subparagraph (1)(b)(iii) is equal to 33.33% of the contributor’s second additional monthly pensionable earnings calculated as provided in subsection (4.02).
Section 56 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):
For the purpose of subsection (3.1), a contributor’s first additional monthly pensionable earnings are an amount calculated by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case where their first additional contributory period is less than or equal to 480 months, dividing their total first additional pensionable earnings by 480; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case where their first additional contributory period exceeds 480 months, dividing the aggregate of their 480 highest first additional pensionable earnings for a month by 480.

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of subsection (3.2), a contributor’s second additional monthly pensionable earnings are an amount calculated by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case where their second additional contributory period is less than or equal to 480 months, dividing their total second additional pensionable earnings by 480; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case where their second additional contributory period exceeds 480 months, dividing the aggregate of their 480 highest second additional pensionable earnings for a month by 480.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 56(5)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in relation to any benefits payable under this Act for any month after December, 1977, any month for which the contributor was a family allowance recipient in a year for which the contributor’s base unadjusted pensionable earnings were equal to or less than the contributor’s basic exemption for the year.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 56(6) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: If division of unadjusted pensionable earnings occurs
Subsection.Text: The amount of the portion of the contributor’s retirement pension that is to be used for the purpose of subparagraph (1)(b)(i), in the case of a contributor in respect of whom a division of unadjusted pensionable earnings takes place either before or after the commencement of the disability pension, if the division reduces the disability pension otherwise payable, is to be calculated by dividing
The portion of subparagraph 56(6)(a)(i) of the Act before clause (A) is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of the portion of the contributor’s retirement pension, which amount is calculated in accordance with subsections (3), (4), (4.1) and (5) before the division, multiplied by the aggregate of

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 56(6)(a)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of the portion of the contributor’s retirement pension, which amount is calculated in accordance with subsections (3), (4), (4.1) and (5) following the division, multiplied by the number of months in the contributor’s contributory period calculated in accordance with subsection (5)

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 58(1)(a)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: 37.5% of the amount of the contributor’s retirement pension, which amount is calculated as the aggregate of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the amount calculated as provided in subsection (3),

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the amount calculated as provided in subsection (3.1), and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: the amount calculated as provided in subsection (3.4),

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 58(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a survivor who has reached 65 years of age and to whom no retirement pension is payable under this Act or a provincial pension plan, a basic monthly amount equal to 60% of the amount of the contributor’s retirement pension, which amount is calculated as the aggregate of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount calculated as provided in subsection (3),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount calculated as provided in subsection (3.1), and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount calculated as provided in subsection (3.4).

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs 58(2)(a)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the aggregate of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: a flat rate benefit, calculated as provided in subsection (1.1), and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the lesser of

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is 37.5% of the amount of the portion of the contributor’s retirement pension, which amount is calculated as provided in subsection (3), and
is the lesser of

FormulaParagraph.Text: 40% of C, and

40% of the amount of the portion of the survivor’s retirement pension, which amount is calculated as provided in paragraph 46(1)(a), without regard to subsections 46(3) to (6), and adjusted in accordance with subsection 45(2), and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: an amount that, when added to the amount of the portion of the survivor’s retirement pension that is calculated as provided in paragraph 46(1)(a), without regard to subsections 46(3) to (6), and adjusted in accordance with subsection 45(2), is equal to the amount of a benefit of 25% of 1/12 of the survivor’s Maximum Pensionable Earnings Average for the later of the year in which the survivor first became qualified to receive the survivor’s pension and the year in which the survivor’s retirement pension commenced to be payable, adjusted in accordance with subsection 45(2) as if the benefit had commenced to be payable in the later of the year in which the survivor first became qualified to receive the survivor’s pension and the year in which the survivor’s retirement pension commenced to be payable,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is 37.5% of the amount of the portion of the contributor’s retirement pension, which amount is calculated as provided in subsection (3.1), and
is the lesser of

FormulaParagraph.Text: 40% of C, and

40% of the amount of the portion of the survivor’s retirement pension, which amount is calculated as provided in paragraph 46(1)(b), without regard to subsections 46(3.1) to (6), and adjusted in accordance with subsection 45(2), and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is 37.5% of the amount of the portion of the contributor’s retirement pension, which amount is calculated as provided in subsection (3.4), and
is the lesser of

FormulaParagraph.Text: 40% of C, and

40% of the amount of the portion of the survivor’s retirement pension, which amount is calculated as provided in paragraph 46(1)(c), without regard to subsections 46(3.1) to (6) and adjusted in accordance with subsection 45(2);

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 58(2)(c) and (d) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a survivor who has reached 65 years of age and who was born after December 31, 1932 and whose retirement pension commences to be payable after December 31, 1997, the aggregate of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the lesser of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is 60% of the amount of the portion of the contributor’s retirement pension, which amount is calculated as provided in subsection (3), and
is the lesser of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (I)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 40% of A, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (II)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 40% of the amount of the portion of the survivor’s retirement pension, which amount is calculated as provided in paragraph 46(1)(a), without regard to subsections 46(3) to (6), and adjusted in accordance with subsection 45(2), and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: an amount that, when added to the amount of the portion of the survivor’s retirement pension that is calculated as provided in paragraph 46(1)(a), without regard to subsections 46(3) to (6), and adjusted in accordance with subsection 45(2), is equal to the amount of a benefit of 25% of 1/12 of the survivor’s Maximum Pensionable Earnings Average for the later of the year in which the survivor first became qualified to receive the survivor’s pension and the year in which the survivor’s retirement pension commenced to be payable, adjusted in accordance with subsection 45(2) as if the benefit had commenced to be payable in the later of the year in which the survivor first became qualified to receive the survivor’s pension and the year in which the survivor’s retirement pension commenced to be payable,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is 60% of the amount of the portion of the contributor’s retirement pension, which amount is calculated as provided in subsection (3.1), and
is the lesser of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (I)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 40% of A, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (II)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 40% of the amount of the portion of the survivor’s retirement pension, which amount is calculated as provided in paragraph 46(1)(b), without regard to subsections 46(3.1) to (6), and adjusted in accordance with subsection 45(2), and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is 60% of the amount of the portion of the contributor’s retirement pension, which amount is calculated as provided in subsection (3.4), and
is the lesser of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (I)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 40% of A, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (II)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 40% of the amount of the portion of the survivor’s retirement pension, which amount is calculated as provided in paragraph 46(1)(c), without regard to subsections 46(3.1) to (6), and adjusted in accordance with subsection 45(2);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, the aggregate of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the lesser of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: 60% of the amount of the portion of the contributor’s retirement pension, which amount is calculated as provided in subsection (3), and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: an amount that, when added to the amount of the portion of the survivor’s retirement pension that is calculated as provided in paragraph 46(1)(a), without regard to subsections 46(3) to (6), and adjusted in accordance with subsection 45(2), is equal to the amount of a benefit of 25% of 1/12 of the average of the Year’s Maximum Pensionable Earnings for the later of the year in which the survivor first became qualified to receive the survivor’s pension and the year in which the survivor’s retirement pension commenced to be payable, and for each of the two preceding years, adjusted in accordance with subsection 45(2) as if the benefit had commenced to be payable in the later of the year in which the survivor first became qualified to receive the survivor’s pension and the year in which the survivor’s retirement pension commenced to be payable,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: 60% of the amount of the portion of the contributor’s retirement pension, which amount is calculated as provided in subsection (3.1), and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: 60% of the amount of the portion of the contributor’s retirement pension, which amount is calculated as provided in subsection (3.4).

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 58(3) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Calculation of portion of contributor’s retirement pension
Subsection.Text: The amount of the portion of the contributor’s retirement pension that is to be used for the purposes of subsections (1) and (2) is an amount calculated as provided in paragraph 57(2)(a), (b) or (c), multiplied, for the purpose of calculating the monthly amount of the survivor’s pension for months commencing with the month in which
Section 58 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Calculation of portion of contributor’s retirement pension
Subsection.Text: The amount of the portion of the contributor’s retirement pension that is to be used for the purposes of subsections (1) and (2) is an amount calculated as provided in subsection (3.2), multiplied, for the purpose of calculating the monthly amount of the survivor’s pension for months commencing with the month in which one of the events described in paragraphs 3(a) to (e) has occurred, by the ratio that the Pension Index for the year that includes that month bears to the Pension Index for the year in which the contributor died.
The amount to be calculated for the purpose of subsection (3.1) is equal to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if a retirement pension was not payable for the month in which the contributor died, an amount equal to 8.33% of their first additional monthly pensionable earnings, or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if a retirement pension was payable for the month in which the contributor died, an amount equal to the product obtained by multiplying

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an amount equal to 8.33% of their first additional monthly pensionable earnings,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the ratio that the Pension Index for the year that includes that month bears to the Pension Index for the year in which the retirement pension first became payable.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Calculation of first additional monthly pensionable earnings
Subsection.Text: For the purpose of subsection (3.2), the first additional monthly pensionable earnings of a contributor are an amount calculated as provided in section 48.1, and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a contributor to whom a retirement pension was payable for the month in which they died, section 51 applies; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a contributor to whom no retirement pension was payable for the month in which they died, section 51 applies but the reference in that section to the year in which a benefit becomes payable to the contributor is to be read as a reference to the year in which the contributor died.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Calculation of portion of contributor’s retirement pension
Subsection.Text: The amount of the portion of the contributor’s retirement pension that is to be used for the purposes of subsections (1) and (2) is an amount calculated as provided in subsection (3.5), multiplied, for the purpose of calculating the monthly amount of the survivor’s pension for months commencing with the month in which one of the events described in paragraphs 3(a) to (e) has occurred, by the ratio that the Pension Index for the year that includes that month bears to the Pension Index for the year in which the contributor died.
The amount that is to be calculated for the purpose of subsection (3.4) is equal to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if a retirement pension was not payable for the month in which the contributor died, an amount equal to 33.33% of their second additional monthly pensionable earnings, or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if a retirement pension was payable for the month in which the contributor died, an amount equal to the product obtained by multiplying

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an amount equal to 33.33% of their second additional monthly pensionable earnings,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the ratio that the Pension Index for the year that includes that month bears to the Pension Index for the year in which the retirement pension first became payable.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Calculation of second additional monthly pensionable earnings
Subsection.Text: For the purpose of subsection (3.5), the second additional monthly pensionable earnings of a contributor are an amount calculated as provided in section 48.2, and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a contributor to whom a retirement pension was payable for the month in which they died, section 51 applies; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a contributor to whom no retirement pension was payable for the month in which they died, section 51 applies but the reference in that section to the year in which a benefit becomes payable to the contributor is to be read as a reference to the year in which the contributor died.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 58(6)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the aggregate of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the greater of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the flat rate benefit payable under subparagraph (1)(a)(i), and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the flat rate benefit payable under paragraph 56(1)(a), and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the lesser of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the aggregate of

Subsubclause.Text: the amount that would have been payable under subparagraph (1)(a)(ii) if the amounts referred to in clauses (1)(a)(ii)(B) and (C) had not been included in the calculation made under that subparagraph, and

the amount that would have been payable under paragraph 56(1)(b) if the amounts referred to in subparagraphs 56(1)(b)(ii) and (iii) had not been included in the calculation made under that paragraph, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: 60% of the lesser of the amount described in sub-subclause (I)(1) and the amount described in sub-subclause (I)(2), and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: 75% of the amount of a benefit of 25% of 1/12 of the survivor’s Maximum Pensionable Earnings Average for the later of the year in which the survivor first became qualified to receive the survivor’s pension and the year in which the survivor’s disability pension commenced to be payable, adjusted in accordance with subsection 45(2) as if the benefit had commenced to be payable in the later of the year in which the survivor first became qualified to receive the survivor’s pension and the year in which the survivor’s disability pension commenced to be payable,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the aggregate of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the greater of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the amount that would have been payable under subparagraph (1)(a)(ii) if the amounts referred to in clauses (1)(a)(ii)(A) and (C) had not been included in the calculation made under that subparagraph, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the amount that would have been payable under paragraph 56(1)(b) if the amounts referred to in subparagraphs 56(1)(b)(i) and (iii) had not been included in the calculation made under that paragraph, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: 60% of the lesser of the amount described in subclause (A) and the amount described in subclause (B), and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the aggregate of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the greater of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the amount that would have been payable under subparagraph (1)(a)(ii) if the amounts referred to in clauses (1)(a)(ii)(A) and (B) had not been included in the calculation made under that subparagraph, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the amount that would have been payable under paragraph 56(1)(b) if the amounts referred to in subparagraphs 56(1)(b)(i) and (ii) had not been included in the calculation made under that paragraph, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: 60% of the lesser of the amount described in subclause (A) and the amount described in subclause (B).

Subsection.Text: Subsection 58(8) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (a), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: 37.5% of the amount of the contributor’s retirement pension, which amount is calculated as the aggregate of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount calculated as provided in subsection (3.1), and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount calculated as provided in subsection (3.4).

Subsection.Text: Subsection 59.1(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Amount of post-retirement benefit

Subsection.Text: A post-retirement benefit payable to a contributor is a basic monthly amount that is equal to the aggregate of the amounts calculated as provided in subsections (1.1), (3) and (5).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Calculation of portion of post-retirement benefit
Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (2) and (7), the amount that is to be used for the purpose of subsection (1) is determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined under subsection 53(1) for the year before the year in which the post-retirement benefit commences to be payable;
is the Year’s Maximum Pensionable Earnings for the year before the year in which the post-retirement benefit commences to be payable;
is the Maximum Pensionable Earnings Average for the year in which the post-retirement benefit commences to be payable;

is the adjustment factor referred to in subsection 46(3) or (3.1), as the case may be, based on the age of the contributor on January 1 of the year in which the post-retirement benefit commences to be payable; and
is the amount determined by the formula
is the amount of the earnings referred to in subparagraph 53(1)(b)(i), and
is the aggregate of the earnings referred to in subparagraph 53(1)(b)(i) and those referred to in subparagraph 53(1)(b)(ii).

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 59.1(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Base unadjusted pensionable earnings for year retirement pension becomes payable
Subsection.Text: For the purpose of the calculation under subsection (1.1), if the contributor’s base unadjusted pensionable earnings are earned in the year in which the contributory period ends under subparagraph 49(b)(iii), the amount determined for A in that subsection is the greater of
Subsection 59.1(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Calculation of portion of post-retirement benefit
Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (4) and (7), the amount that is to be used for the purpose of subsection (1) is determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined under subsection 53.1(1) for the year before the year in which the post-retirement benefit commences to be payable, except that

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the post-retirement benefit commences in 2020, A is the amount for 2019, multiplied by 0.15,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the post-retirement benefit commences in 2021, A is the amount for 2020, multiplied by 0.3,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (c)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the post-retirement benefit commences in 2022, A is the amount for 2021, multiplied by 0.5, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (d)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the post-retirement benefit commences in 2023, A is the amount for 2022, multiplied by 0.75;

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the Year’s Maximum Pensionable Earnings for the year before the year in which the post-retirement benefit commences to be payable;
is the Maximum Pensionable Earnings Average for the year in which the post-retirement benefit commences to be payable; and
is the adjustment factor referred to in subsection 46(3.1), based on the age of the contributor on January 1 of the year in which the post-retirement benefit commences to be payable.

Subsection.MarginalNote: First additional unadjusted pensionable earnings for year retirement pension becomes payable
Subsection.Text: For the purpose of the calculation under subsection (3), if the contributor’s first additional unadjusted pensionable earnings are earned in the year in which the first additional contributory period ends under paragraph 49.1(c), the amount determined for A in that subsection is the greater of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: zero, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount that is calculated by subtracting the Year’s Maximum Pensionable Earnings for that year — multiplied by the number of months in the year before the retirement pension becomes payable and divided by 12 — from the amount determined under subsection 53.1(1).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Calculation of portion of post-retirement benefit
Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (6) and (7), the amount that is to be used for the purpose of subsection (1) is determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined under section 53.2 for the year before the year in which the post-retirement benefit commences to be payable;
is the Year’s Maximum Pensionable Earnings for the year before the year in which the post-retirement benefit commences to be payable;
is the Maximum Pensionable Earnings Average for the year in which the post-retirement benefit commences to be payable; and
is the adjustment factor referred to in subsection 46(3.1), based on the age of the contributor on January 1 of the year in which the post-retirement benefit commences to be payable.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Second additional unadjusted pensionable earnings for year retirement pension becomes payable
Subsection.Text: For the purpose of the calculation under subsection (5), if the contributor’s second additional unadjusted pensionable earnings are earned in the year in which the second additional contributory period ends under paragraph 49.2(c), the amount determined for A in that subsection is the greater of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: zero, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount that is calculated by subtracting the Year’s Additional Maximum Pensionable Earnings for that year — multiplied by the number of months in the year before the retirement pension becomes payable and divided by 12 — from the amount determined under section 53.2.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Adjustment factor for contributors — 70 years of age or older
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of the calculations under subsections (1.1), (3) and (5), if the contributor is 70 years of age or older, the adjustment factor referred to in E in each of those subsections is that of a contributor who is 70 years of age.
Subsection 65.1(8) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: first additional joint contributory period means the period commencing on January 1, 2019 or with the month in which the elder of the two spouses or of the two common-law partners reaches 18 years of age, whichever is later, and ending

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if both spouses or common-law partners are contributors, with the month in which the later of their respective first additional contributory periods ends; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if only one spouse or common-law partner is a contributor, with the later of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the month in which the contributor’s first additional contributory period ends, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the earlier of the month in which the non-contributor reaches 70 years of age and the month in which an application for an assignment of a retirement pension is approved; (première période cotisable conjointe supplémentaire)

Definition.Text: second additional joint contributory period means the period commencing on January 1, 2024 or with the month in which the elder of the two spouses or of the two common-law partners reaches 18 years of age, whichever is later, and ending

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if both spouses or common-law partners are contributors, with the month in which the later of their respective second additional contributory periods ends; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if only one spouse or common-law partner is a contributor, with the later of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the month in which the contributor’s second additional contributory period ends, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the earlier of the month in which the non-contributor reaches 70 years of age and the month in which an application for an assignment of a retirement pension is approved. (deuxième période cotisable conjointe supplémentaire)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 65.1(9) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The portion of a contributor’s retirement pension to be assigned to the contributor’s spouse or common-law partner under this section is an amount equal to the aggregate of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an amount calculated by multiplying

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of the portion of the contributor’s retirement pension calculated as provided in paragraph 46(1)(a) and adjusted in accordance with section 45, by

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: 50% of the ratio that the number of months in the period of cohabitation bears to the number of months in the joint contributory period,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an amount calculated by multiplying

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of the portion of the contributor’s retirement pension, calculated as provided in paragraph 46(1)(b) and adjusted in accordance with section 45, by

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: 50% of the ratio that the number of months in the period of cohabitation bears to the number of months in the first additional joint contributory period, and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an amount calculated by multiplying

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of the portion of the contributor’s retirement pension, calculated as provided in paragraph 46(1)(c) and adjusted in accordance with section 45, by

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: 50% of the ratio that the number of months in the period of cohabitation bears to the number of months in the second additional joint contributory period.

Section.Text: Paragraph 77(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the total pensionable earnings of the contributor attributable to base contributions made under this Act,

Section.Text: The portion of section 78 of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Total pensionable earnings attributable to base contributions made under Act
Section.Text: The total pensionable earnings of a contributor attributable to base contributions made under this Act are an amount equal to the amount that their total pensionable earnings would be if the base unadjusted pensionable earnings of the contributor for a year were that proportion of their base unadjusted pensionable earnings for the year that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the contributor’s earnings on which a base contribution has been made for the year under this Act, calculated as provided in subparagraph 53(1)(b)(i),

Section.Text: Section 79 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Total pensionable earnings attributable to base contributions made under Act as a result of division
Section.Text: For a year of a division as determined under section 55 or 55.1 and under a provincial pension plan, the total pensionable earnings of a contributor attributable to base contributions made under this Act are an amount equal to the amount that their total pensionable earnings would be if the base unadjusted pensionable earnings of the contributor for the year were that proportion of their base unadjusted pensionable earnings for the year that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: their base unadjusted pensionable earnings attributed under subsection 55(4) or 55.2(5)

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: their total base unadjusted pensionable earnings for the year determined under subsection 55(5) or 55.2(6).

Section.Text: Subsection 80(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Provision for making of financial adjustments
Subsection.Text: Any agreement entered into under subsection (1) may provide for the making of any financial adjustments required to be made by reason of any payments made to or in respect of a contributor in accordance with that agreement, and for the crediting or charging of the amount of those adjustments to the Canada Pension Plan Account or the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account, as the case may be.
Paragraph 89(1)(e) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the determination of disability subject to this Part and the conditions on which any amount as or on account of a benefit in respect of the disability of a person shall be paid or shall continue to be paid, including the initial and subsequent periodic or other assessments of that disability and the reasonable rehabilitation measures to be undergone by that person, and providing for the payment out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund of the cost of any such assessments of disability and rehabilitation measures and for the charging of the amount of the payment to the Canada Pension Plan Account or the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account, as the case may be, as a cost of administration of this Act;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 89(1)(j) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: providing, in any case or class of cases not covered by the provisions of an agreement under subsection 80(1), for the issue of cheques by the Government of Canada in the amount of any benefit payable under this Act to or in respect of a contributor and in the amount of any like benefit payable under a provincial pension plan to or in respect of the same contributor, or for the payment by other means by the Government of Canada of such an amount, if arrangements satisfactory to the Governor in Council have been made with the government of that province for the issue of cheques, or for the payment by other means, by that government on a reciprocal basis and for the making of any financial adjustments by that government required to be made by reason of those arrangements, and providing for the making of any financial adjustments by the Government of Canada required to be made by reason of those arrangements and for the crediting or charging of the amount of the adjustments to the Canada Pension Plan Account or the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account, as the case may be;

Section.Text: Section 91 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: additional Canada Pension Plan means the part of the Canada Pension Plan relating to the portions of benefits that are referred to in paragraphs 46(1)(b) and (c), subparagraphs 56(1)(b)(ii) and (iii), clauses 58(1)(a)(ii)(B) and (C), subparagraphs 58(1)(b)(ii) and (iii) and subsections 59.1(3) and (5) and all contributions in respect of those portions of benefits. (régime de pensions supplémentaire du Canada)

base Canada Pension Plan means the part of the Canada Pension Plan relating to benefits and contributions under this Act, other than the portions of those benefits and contributions that are included in the additional Canada Pension Plan. (régime de pensions de base du Canada)

Section.Text: Subsection 98(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every individual who is required by section 30 to file a return of their self-employed earnings for a year, other than an individual to whom subsection (1) or (2) applies, shall on or before the first day on or before which they are required by section 33 to pay any amount as or on account of the contributions required to be made by them for that year in respect of those earnings, if they have not earlier been assigned a Social Insurance Number, apply to the Minister, in the form and manner that may be prescribed, for the assignment to them of a Social Insurance Number.

Section.Text: Paragraph 99(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the individual is not employed in pensionable employment but later becomes so employed, or is required to make a contribution under this Act in respect of their self-employed earnings, within 60 days after the day on which the individual becomes so employed or after the first day on or before which they are required under section 33 to pay any amount as or on account of the contributions required to be made by them in respect of those earnings, as the case may be.

Section.Text: Subsection 107(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Regulations for giving effect to agreements
Subsection.Text: For the purpose of giving effect to any agreement entered into under subsection (1), the Governor in Council may make any regulations respecting the manner in which this Act is to apply to any case or class of cases affected by the agreement, and for adapting this Act to any such case or class of cases, that appear to the Governor in Council to be necessary for that purpose, and any regulations so made may provide for the making of any financial adjustments required under the agreement and for the crediting or charging of the amount of any of those adjustments to the Canada Pension Plan Account or the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account, as the case may be.
Paragraph 108(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: all amounts received under this Act as or on account of contributions under subsections 8(1), 9(1) and 10(1) or otherwise on account of the base Canada Pension Plan;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 108(2)(c) to (g) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any amount of money received under section 107.1 on account of the base Canada Pension Plan and any proceeds from the disposition of any securities or other property received under that section on account of the base Canada Pension Plan;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: all amounts charged for the use of resources that are associated with the administration of this Act in relation to the base Canada Pension Plan;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: any interest or administrative charge collected in relation to money payable under this Act in relation to the base Canada Pension Plan; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: all amounts received under subsection 56(1) of the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board Act.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 108(3)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: all amounts payable under this Act in relation to the base Canada Pension Plan as or on account of benefits or otherwise;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 108(3)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the costs of administration of this Act in relation to the base Canada Pension Plan, under the authority of Parliament;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 108(3)(e) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the costs, in relation to the base Canada Pension Plan, of administering Part 5 of the Department of Employment and Social Development Act in respect of appeals respecting this Act.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 108(4)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the fair market value of the assets of the Investment Board less its liabilities, in relation to the base Canada Pension Plan.

Section.Text: Subsection 108.1(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, by notice, and in accordance with any agreement entered into under section 111.1, require the Investment Board to pay into the Consolidated Revenue Fund any amount necessary to offset amounts charged or required to be charged to the Canada Pension Plan Account under subsection 108(3) and any interest charged under subsection 110(1).

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 108.1:

Section.MarginalNote: Additional Canada Pension Plan Account

Subsection.Text: There is established in the accounts of Canada an account to be known as the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Amounts to be credited to Account
Subsection.Text: There is to be paid into the Consolidated Revenue Fund and credited to the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: all amounts received under this Act as or on account of contributions under subsections 8(1.1) and (1.2), 9(1.1) and (1.2) and 10(1.1) and (1.2) or otherwise on account of the additional Canada Pension Plan;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: all amounts required to be credited to the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account under any agreement entered into under subsection 39(1) or 80(1) or under any regulation made under paragraph 89(1)(j) or subsection 107(3);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any amount of money received under section 107.1 on account of the additional Canada Pension Plan and any proceeds from the disposition of any securities or other property received under that section on account of the additional Canada Pension Plan;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: all amounts charged for the use of resources that are associated with the administration of this Act in relation to the additional Canada Pension Plan;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: any interest or administrative charge collected in relation to money payable under this Act in relation to the additional Canada Pension Plan; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: all amounts received under subsection 56(1.1) of the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board Act.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Amounts to be charged to Account
Subsection.Text: There is to be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund and charged to the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: all amounts payable under this Act in relation to the additional Canada Pension Plan as or on account of benefits or otherwise;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: all amounts required to be charged to the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account under any agreement entered into under subsection 39(1) or 80(1) or under any regulation made under paragraph 89(1)(j) or subsection 107(3);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: all amounts credited to the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account under paragraph (2)(d);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the costs of administration of this Act in relation to the additional Canada Pension Plan, under the authority of Parliament;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: all amounts required to be charged to the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account under section 57.1 of the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the costs, in relation to the additional Canada Pension Plan, of administering Part 5 of the Department of Employment and Social Development Act in respect of appeals respecting this Act.

Subsection.Text: No payment is to be made out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund under this section in excess of the total of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of the balance to the credit of the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the fair market value of the assets of the Investment Board less its liabilities, in relation to the additional Canada Pension Plan.

Section.MarginalNote: Management of Account

Subsection.Text: Any amounts standing to the credit of the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account that exceed the immediate obligations of that Account are to be transferred to the Investment Board, unless any agreement entered into under section 111.1 provides otherwise. The amounts are to be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund and charged to the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account.

The Minister may, by notice, and in accordance with any agreement entered into under section 111.1, require the Investment Board to pay into the Consolidated Revenue Fund any amount necessary to offset amounts charged or required to be charged to the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account under subsection 108.2(3) and any interest charged under subsection 110(2).

The Minister of Finance shall credit interest to the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account at market rates, as determined by that Minister, on any amount standing to the credit of that Account. The interest is to be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund.

Section.Text: Section 110 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Interest — Canada Pension Plan Account

Subsection.Text: The Minister of Finance shall charge interest to the Canada Pension Plan Account at market rates, as determined by that Minister, on any amount paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund under subsection 108(3) that exceeds the balance to the credit of the Canada Pension Plan Account. Interest is to be charged for the period beginning on the day on which the amount is paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund under subsection 108(3) and ending on the day on which the Investment Board pays that amount into the Consolidated Revenue Fund under subsection 56(1) of the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board Act.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Interest — Additional Canada Pension Plan Account
Subsection.Text: The Minister of Finance shall charge interest to the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account at market rates, as determined by that Minister, on any amount paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund under subsection 108.2(3) that exceeds the balance to the credit of the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account. Interest is to be charged for the period beginning on the day on which the amount is paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund under subsection 108.2(3) and ending on the day on which the Investment Board pays that amount into the Consolidated Revenue Fund under subsection 56(1.1) of the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board Act.

Section.Text: Subsection 112(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Annual financial statements

Subsection.Text: The Minister shall, as soon as possible after the end of each fiscal year, prepare one set of annual financial statements for the Canada Pension Plan in respect of that year setting out

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a statement combining the amounts credited to or charged to the Canada Pension Plan Account, the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account and the accounts of the Investment Board during the year;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a schedule combining the amounts credited to or charged to the Canada Pension Plan Account and the accounts of the Investment Board in relation to the base Canada Pension Plan during the year;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a schedule combining the amounts credited to or charged to the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account and the accounts of the Investment Board in relation to the additional Canada Pension Plan during the year;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a statement combining the accounts of the Canada Pension Plan Account, the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account and the Investment Board as at the end of the year;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a schedule combining the accounts of the Canada Pension Plan Account and the Investment Board in relation to the base Canada Pension Plan as at the end of the year;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a schedule combining the accounts of the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account and the Investment Board in relation to the additional Canada Pension Plan as at the end of the year; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: any other accounts and information that the Minister considers appropriate to present fairly the financial transactions and the financial position of the Canada Pension Plan for the year.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 113(2)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the total amount of all contributions credited to the Canada Pension Plan Account and the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account, to the day on which the regulation referred to in subsection (1) became effective, in respect of employment in that province or in respect of self-employed earnings of persons resident in that province, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the part of the net investment return of the Investment Board and all interest credited to or accrued to the credit of the Canada Pension Plan Account and the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account, to the day on which the regulation referred to in subsection (1) became effective, that is derived from the contributions referred to in paragraph (a),

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 113(2)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the part of the costs of administration of this Act, to the day on which the regulation referred to in subsection (1) became effective, that is equal to the proportion of those costs that the total amount of the contributions referred to in paragraph (a) is of the total amount of all contributions credited to the Canada Pension Plan Account and the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account to that day.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 113.1(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Review every three years

Subsection.Text: Once every three years after 1997, the Minister of Finance and ministers of the Crown from the included provinces shall review the financial state of the Canada Pension Plan and may make recommendations as to whether benefits, contribution rates, first additional contribution rates or second additional contribution rates should be changed.
Subparagraphs 113.1(4)(b)(i) to (iv) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the outstanding balance of the Canada Pension Plan Account and the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the projected revenues into and payments out of the Canada Pension Plan Account and the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the ratio of the projected assets over the projected expenditures of the base Canada Pension Plan and the additional Canada Pension Plan, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the changes, if any, to the amounts and ratios projected at the previous review under this section attributable to changing demographic and economic circumstances or to changes to the base Canada Pension Plan or the additional Canada Pension Plan affecting payments or contributions under the Canada Pension Plan;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 113.1(4)(c) and (d) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the financing objective, for the base Canada Pension Plan, of having a contribution rate, without taking into account the changes, if any, referred to in paragraph (e) for which the contribution rate most recently calculated under subparagraph 115(1.1)(c)(ii) exceeds zero, that is no lower than the rate

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: that, beginning with the year 2003, is the lowest constant rate that can be maintained over the foreseeable future, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: that results in the ratio of the projected assets of the base Canada Pension Plan at the end of any given year over the projected expenditures of the base Canada Pension Plan in the following year being generally constant;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the financing objective, for the additional Canada Pension Plan, of having additional contribution rates, without taking into account the changes, if any, referred to in paragraph (e) for which the additional contribution rates most recently calculated under subparagraphs 115(1.1)(d)(ii) and (e)(ii) exceed zero, that are no lower than the rates

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: that, beginning with the year 2024, are the lowest constant rates that can be maintained over the foreseeable future, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: that result in projected contributions and investment income that are sufficient to fully pay the projected expenditures of the additional Canada Pension Plan over the foreseeable future; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: that changes to the Act that increase benefits or add new benefits must be accompanied by a permanent increase in the contribution rates under this Act to cover the extra costs of the increased or new benefits and by a temporary increase in the contribution rates under this Act for a number of years that is consistent with common actuarial practice to fully pay any unfunded liability resulting from the increased or new benefits.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 113.1(5) to (7) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Recommendations made on completion of review
Subsection.Text: On the completion of a review required by subsection (1), the Minister of Finance may recommend to the Governor in Council that the Governor in Council make regulations under subsection (6) to amend Schedule 1 or 2 to give effect to any recommendations made under subsection (1). If the recommendations made under subsection (1) are that no changes be made to benefits, contribution rates, first additional contributions rates or second additional contribution rates, the Minister of Finance shall cause those recommendations to be published in the Canada Gazette.

Subject to subsections (7) and (8), the Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister of Finance made under subsection (5), by regulation amend Schedule 1 or 2 to change the contribution rates, first additional contribution rates or second additional contribution rates for any or all of the years following the review.
The following shall apply with respect to any changes to and setting of the rates under subsection (6):

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the rates for employees and employers for a year must be identical;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the rates for self-employed persons for a year must be equal to the sum of the rates for employees and employers for that year;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: no rate for employees and employers for a year may be increased by more than one-tenth of a percentage point above the rate for the previous year; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: no rate for self-employed persons for a year may be increased by more than two-tenths of a percentage point above the rate for the previous year.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 113.1(11.05)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of the benefits payable in respect of the base Canada Pension Plan in the three-year period shall be determined as if the ratios referred to in paragraphs 45(2)(b) and 56(2)(c), subsection 58(1.1) and subparagraph 59(c)(ii) were each 1; and

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 113.1(11.05)(b) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: Schedule 1 is deemed to have been amended as of the next day after that October 1

Subsection.Text: Subsections 113.1(11.15) and (12) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Deemed changes to rates — additional Canada Pension Plan
Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (11.143), if, at October 1 of the year before a three-year period for which a review is required by subsection (1), either of the following conditions is met, Schedule 2 is deemed to have been amended as of the next day after that October 1 to change the first additional contribution rates or second additional contribution rates, if required, in accordance with the calculations set out in the regulations:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the difference between the first additional contribution rate most recently calculated under paragraph 115(1.1)(d) and the first additional contribution rate for self-employed persons set out in Schedule 2 for a prescribed year is not within the range set out in the regulations; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the difference between the second additional contribution rate most recently calculated under paragraph 115(1.1)(e) and the second additional contribution rate for self-employed persons set out in Schedule 2 for a prescribed year is not within the range set out in the regulations.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Determination of benefits — additional Canada Pension Plan
Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (11.143), if, at October 1 of the year before a three-year period for which a review is required by subsection (1), either of the conditions set out in paragraphs (11.141)(a) and (b) is met, the portions of benefits under this Act in respect of the additional Canada Pension Plan for the three-year period shall be determined in accordance with the regulations.
Subsections (11.141) and (11.142) do not apply if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a recommendation was made under subsection (1) in the three years before the three-year period referred to in subsection (11.141) that the first additional contribution rates or second additional contribution rates for one or more of the years in that three-year period be changed and the rates were changed before October 1 of the year before that three-year period, by an Act of Parliament or by a regulation made under subsection (6), to give effect to that recommendation; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a recommendation was made under subsection (1) in the three years before the three-year period referred to in subsection (11.141) that the first additional contribution rates or second additional contribution rates for the years in that three-year period not be changed and the Minister of Finance before October 1 of the year before that three-year period has caused that recommendation to be published in the Canada Gazette.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister of Finance, make regulations respecting

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the calculation of deemed changes to rates for the purposes of subsection (11.141);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the determination of the ranges referred to in paragraphs (11.141)(a) and (b); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the determination of portions of benefits for the purposes of subsection (11.142).

Subsection.Text: Regulations under subsection (11.144) may only be made if the lieutenant governor in council of each of at least two thirds of the included provinces, having in total not less than two thirds of the population of all of the included provinces, has signified the consent of that province.
The Minister of Finance shall publish in the Canada Gazette any amendment to Schedule 1 or 2 deemed to have been made under this section.
For greater certainty, subsection 114(2) does not apply to any amendment to Schedule 1 or 2 made under subsection (6), (11.05) to (11.11) or (11.141).
Subsection 114(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If any enactment of Parliament contains any provision that alters, or the effect of which is to alter, either directly or indirectly and either immediately or in the future, the general level of benefits provided by this Act or the contribution rate, first additional contribution rate or second additional contribution rate for employees, employers or self-employed persons for any year, it shall be deemed to be a term of that enactment, whether or not it is expressly stated in the enactment, that the provision shall come into force only on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council, which day shall not in any case be earlier than the first day of the third year following the year in which any notice of intention to introduce a measure containing a provision to that effect was laid before Parliament.
Paragraph 114(4)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the contribution rate, first additional contribution rate or second additional contribution rate for employees, employers or self-employed persons for any year,

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 114(4)(e) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the management or operation of the Canada Pension Plan Account or the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account, or

Subsection.Text: Subsection 114(4.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsections (2) and (4) do not apply in respect of changes under any of subsections 113.1(11.05) to (11.11), (11.141) or (11.142) to benefits, contribution rates, first additional contribution rates or second additional contribution rates.
Subsection 115(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Report of Chief Actuary

Subsection.Text: The Chief Actuary of the Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions shall, during the first year of each three-year period for which a review is required by subsection 113.1(1), prepare a report setting out, as at a date not earlier than December 31 of the year before the three-year period, the results of an actuarial examination of the operation of this Act based on the state of the Canada Pension Plan Account, the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account and the investments of the Investment Board.
Paragraph 115(1.1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: state, for each of the 30 years immediately following the date of the examination,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the estimated revenues of the Canada Pension Plan Account and the estimated investment income of the Investment Board in respect of the base Canada Pension Plan, and the estimated amount of all payments under subsection 108(3), and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the estimated revenues of the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account and the estimated investment income of the Investment Board in respect of the additional Canada Pension Plan, and the estimated amount of all payments under subsection 108.2(3);

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 115(1.1)(c) to (d) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of the base Canada Pension Plan, specify a contribution rate calculated, in respect of self-employed persons for each year of a period of not less than 75 years after the three-year period in which the report is prepared, by combining

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a contribution rate, calculated in the prescribed manner, without taking into account the changes, if any, referred to in paragraph 113.1(4)(e) for which the contribution rate most recently calculated under subparagraph (ii) exceeds zero, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a contribution rate calculated in the prescribed manner in respect of the changes, if any, referred to in paragraph 113.1(4)(e) in respect of the base Canada Pension Plan;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of the additional Canada Pension Plan, specify a first additional contribution rate calculated, in respect of self-employed persons for each year of a period of not less than 75 years after the three-year period in which the report is prepared, by combining

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a first additional contribution rate, calculated in the prescribed manner, without taking into account the changes, if any, referred to in paragraph 113.1(4)(e) for which the first additional contribution rate most recently calculated under subparagraph (ii) exceeds zero, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a first additional contribution rate calculated in the prescribed manner in respect of the changes, if any, referred to in paragraph 113.1(4)(e) that affect the first additional contribution rate;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of the additional Canada Pension Plan, specify a second additional contribution rate calculated, in respect of self-employed persons for each year of a period of not less than 75 years after the three-year period in which the report is prepared, by combining

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a second additional contribution rate, calculated in the prescribed manner, without taking into account the changes, if any, referred to in paragraph 113.1(4)(e) for which the second additional contribution rate most recently calculated under subparagraph (ii) exceeds zero, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a second additional contribution rate calculated in the prescribed manner in respect of the changes, if any, referred to in paragraph 113.1(4)(e) that affect the second additional contribution rate;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: specify the rates referred to in subparagraphs (c)(i) and (ii), (d)(i) and (ii) and (e)(i) and (ii) and set out the manner in which those rates were calculated.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 115(1.2) and (1.3) of the Act are replaced by the following:
For the purposes of the calculations referred to in paragraphs (1.1)(c) to (e),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the rates for employees and employers for a year must be identical; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the rates for self-employed persons for a year must be equal to the sum of the rates for employees and employers for that year.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 114(4) applies, with any modifications that the circumstances require, to the making of the regulations prescribing the manner of the calculation referred to in paragraphs (1.1)(c) to (e) and to the making of any regulation changing that manner of calculation.

Section.Text: Subsection 118(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Government contributions — Canada Pension Plan Account

Subsection.Text: There is to be charged to the Consolidated Revenue Fund and credited to the Canada Pension Plan Account an amount equal to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the contributions required to be made by Her Majesty in right of Canada under subsection 9(1) in respect of persons in employment under Her Majesty in right of Canada that is not excepted employment under this Act, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount required by subsection 21(2) to be paid by Her Majesty in right of Canada as a result of the failure to deduct and remit, in accordance with this Act, the required amount as or on account of contributions required to be made under subsection 8(1) by persons referred to in paragraph (a).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Government contributions — Additional Canada Pension Plan Account
Subsection.Text: There is to be charged to the Consolidated Revenue Fund and credited to the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account an amount equal to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the contributions required to be made by Her Majesty in right of Canada under subsections 9(1.1) and (1.2) in respect of persons in employment under Her Majesty in right of Canada that is not excepted employment under this Act, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount required by subsection 21(2) to be paid by Her Majesty in right of Canada as a result of the failure to deduct and remit, in accordance with this Act, the required amount as or on account of contributions required to be made under subsections 8(1.1) and (1.2) by persons referred to in paragraph (a).

Section.Text: The schedule to the Act is renumbered as Schedule 1.
Schedule 1 to the Act is amended by replacing the references after the heading “SCHEDULE 1” with the following:

AmendedText.OriginatingRef: (Subsections 11.1(2) and 113.1(5) and (6), paragraph 113.1(11.05)(b) and subsections 113.1(11.15) and (12))

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding, after Schedule 1, the Schedule 2 that is set out in the schedule to this Act.

Heading.TitleText: Canada Pension Plan Investment Board Act

Section.Text: Paragraph 5(b) of the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to manage any amounts transferred to it under sections 108.1 and 108.3 of the Canada Pension Plan, and its right, title or interest in any designated securities, in the best interests of the contributors and beneficiaries under that Act; and

Section.Text: Section 39 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (7):

Subsection.Text: The financial statements required to be prepared under this section shall be prepared, as one set of statements, jointly and separately in relation to amounts managed by the Board for the purposes of the base Canada Pension Plan and the additional Canada Pension Plan, as defined in section 91 of the Canada Pension Plan.

Section.Text: The heading before section 56 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Heading.TitleText: Canada Pension Plan Account and Additional Canada Pension Plan Account

Section.Text: Subsection 56(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Duty of Board — Canada Pension Plan Account

Subsection.Text: The Board shall pay into the Consolidated Revenue Fund, for credit to the Canada Pension Plan Account established under subsection 108(1) of the Canada Pension Plan,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any amount required under subsection 108.1(2) of that Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any amount required under subsection 113(1.1) of that Act in relation to the base Canada Pension Plan, as defined in section 91 of that Act.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Duty of Board — Additional Canada Pension Plan Account
Subsection.Text: The Board shall pay into the Consolidated Revenue Fund, for credit to the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account established under subsection 108.2(1) of the Canada Pension Plan,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any amount required under subsection 108.3(2) of that Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any amount required under subsection 113(1.1) of that Act in relation to the additional Canada Pension Plan, as defined in section 91 of that Act.

Section.Text: Section 57 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Administration costs — Canada Pension Plan Account
Section.Text: If the Minister is of the opinion that the Board cannot pay its administration costs in relation to the base Canada Pension Plan, as defined in section 91 of the Canada Pension Plan, the Minister shall pay those costs out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund and the payment shall be charged to the Canada Pension Plan Account established under subsection 108(1) of that Act.

Section.MarginalNote: Administration costs — Additional Canada Pension Plan Account
Section.Text: If the Minister is of the opinion that the Board cannot pay its administration costs in relation to the additional Canada Pension Plan, as defined in section 91 of the Canada Pension Plan, the Minister shall pay those costs out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund and the payment shall be charged to the Additional Canada Pension Plan Account established under subsection 108.2(1) of that Act.

Section.MarginalNote: Definitions
Section.Text: The following definitions apply in sections 63 and 64.

Definition.Text: additional Canada Pension Plan has the same meaning as in section 91 of the Canada Pension Plan. (régime de pensions supplémentaire du Canada)
Additional Canada Pension Plan Account means the account established under subsection 108.2(1) of the Canada Pension Plan. (compte supplémentaire du régime de pensions du Canada)
Canada Pension Plan Account means the account established under subsection 108(1) of the Canada Pension Plan. (compte du régime de pensions du Canada)
Minister means the Minister of Employment and Social Development. (ministre)

Section.MarginalNote: Initial costs of administration

Subsection.Text: Despite paragraphs 108.2(3)(c), (d) and (f) of the Canada Pension Plan, until a date determined by the Minister, the costs of administration in relation to the additional Canada Pension Plan must be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund and charged to the Canada Pension Plan Account. The date determined by the Minister must not be later than December 31, 2020.

The Minister of Finance must calculate interest, at rates determined by that Minister, on any amount paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund under subsection (1). Interest must be calculated for the period beginning on the day on which the amount is paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund under subsection (1) and ending on the day on which the amount is credited to the Canada Pension Plan Account under subsection (3).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Amounts credited to Canada Pension Plan Account
